testing_centers.notes: Used by most states for insurance licensing exams. Both in-person testing centers and online proctoring available.
Used by select states for insurance licensing exams. Both in-person testing centers and online proctoring available.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name on ID must match exam registration exactly.
exam_rules: Closed-book; no personal notes or reference materials permitted. On-screen calculator provided for some states. Passing score typically 70% (varies 65–75% by state). Scratch paper or erasable noteboards may be provided at testing center. No phones or electronic devices permitted in exam room.
renewal_options: License valid for 2 years. Continuing education (CE) required for renewal; typically 24 CE credits per 2-year cycle, but varies by state and line of authority. CE requirements and approved providers managed through each state insurance department and tracked via NIPR (nipr.com).

retake_policy.notes: Retake rules vary by state. Typically 24-hour waiting period between attempts. Most states allow up to 3 attempts before a mandatory waiting period (commonly 6 months) applies. Full exam fee required for each retake attempt.

official_study_resources.title: State Insurance Exam Candidate Handbook
official_study_resources.notes: Free state-specific candidate handbooks available from PSI Exams and Pearson VUE; contains exam content outline and testing policies.
